Effective Date of 2018 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–397, title III, § 305(d)132 Stat. 5324Dec. 21, 2018“The amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall take effect as if included in the enactment of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 [].”Pub. L. 104–1Amendment by effective , and any reference to the Office of Compliance in any law, rule, regulation, or other official paper in effect as of such date to be considered to refer and apply to the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights, see , set out as a note under .section 308(b) of Pub. L. 115–397section 308(d) of Pub. L. 115–397section 1381 of this titleDec. 21, 2018, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–397, title IV, § 401132 Stat. 5327Dec. 21, 2018“(a).—In GeneralExcept as otherwise provided in this Act [See Short Title of 2018 Amendment note below], this Act and the amendments made by this Act shall take effect upon the expiration of the 180-day period which begins on the date of the enactment of this Act [].Dec. 21, 2018“(b).—No Effect on Pending ProceedingsNothing in this Act or the amendments made by this Act may be construed to affect any proceeding or payment of an award or settlement relating to a claim under title IV of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 ( et seq.) which is pending as of the date after that 180-day period. If, as of that date, an employee has begun any of the proceedings under that title that were available to the employee prior to that date, the employee may complete, or initiate and complete, all such proceedings, and such proceedings shall remain in effect with respect to, and provide the exclusive proceedings for, the claim involved until the completion of all such proceedings.”2 U.S.C. 1401, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–141, div. I, title I, § 153(c)132 Stat. 787Mar. 23, 2018“This section [amending this section and sections 1314 to 1316, 1331, 1341, 1351, 1401, 1404, and 1415 of this title] and the amendments made by this section—“(1)shall take effect on the date of enactment of this section []; andMar. 23, 2018“(2)shall apply to any charge, complaint, or claim, that is made on or after the date of enactment of this section, of a violation of—“(A)section 201, 202, 203, 207, or 210 of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 ( et seq.) [, 1312, 1313, 1317, 1331]; or2 U.S.C. 13112 U.S.C. 1311“(B)a direct provision as defined in section 404(a) of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 ([(a)]) (as added by subsection (b)).”2 U.S.C. 1404

children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Directors of the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: 2 Chair
children.text: The term “Chair” means the Chair of the Board of Directors of the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights.

children.label: A
children.text: the House of Representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services;

children.label: D
children.text: the Capitol Police;

children.label: E
children.text: the Congressional Budget Office;

children.label: F
children.text: the Office of the Architect of the Capitol;

children.label: G
children.text: the Office of the Attending Physician;

children.label: H
children.text: the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights;

children.label: I
children.text: the Office of Technology Assessment;

children.label: J
children.text: the Library of Congress, except for ; orsection 1351 of this title

children.label: K
children.text: the John C. Stennis Center for Public Service Training and Development.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: 4 Employee
children.text: The term “employee” includes an applicant for employment and a former employee.

children.heading: Employee of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol
children.label: 5 Employee of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol
children.text: The term “employee of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol” includes any employee of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol or the Botanic Garden.

children.heading: Employee of the Capitol Police
children.label: 6 Employee of the Capitol Police
children.text: The term “employee of the Capitol Police” includes any member or officer of the Capitol Police.

children.heading: Employee of the House of Representatives
children.label: 7 Employee of the House of Representatives
children.text: The term “employee of the House of Representatives” includes an individual occupying a position the pay for which is disbursed by the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, or another official designated by the House of Representatives, or any employment position in an entity that is paid with funds derived from the clerk-hire allowance of the House of Representatives but not any such individual employed by any entity listed in subparagraphs (C) through (K) of paragraph (3).

children.heading: Employee of the Senate
children.label: 8 Employee of the Senate
children.text: The term “employee of the Senate” includes any employee whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary of the Senate, but not any such individual employed by any entity listed in subparagraphs (C) through (K) of paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: the personal office of a Member of the House of Representatives or of a Senator;

children.label: B
children.text: a committee of the House of Representatives or the Senate or a joint committee;

children.label: C
children.text: any other office headed by a person with the final authority to appoint, hire, discharge, and set the terms, conditions, or privileges of the employment of an employee of the House of Representatives or the Senate;

children.label: D
children.text: the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services, the United States Capitol Police, the Congressional Budget Office, the Office of the Architect of the Capitol, the Office of the Attending Physician, the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights, the Office of Technology Assessment, and the John C. Stennis Center for Public Service Training and Development; or

children.label: E
children.text: the Library of Congress, except for .section 1351 of this title

children.heading: Executive Director
children.label: 10 Executive Director
children.text: The term “Executive Director” means the Executive Director of the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights.

children.heading: General Counsel
children.label: 11 General Counsel
children.text: The term “General Counsel” means the General Counsel of the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights.

children.heading: Office
children.label: 12 Office
children.text: The term “Office” means the Office of Congressional Workplace Rights.

children.heading: Clarification of coverage of employees of certain commissions
children.label: b Clarification of coverage of employees of certain commissions
A
children.text: any individual who is an employee of such Commission shall be considered a covered employee for purposes of this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Commission shall be considered an employing office for purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Authority to provide legal assistance and representation
children.label: 2 Authority to provide legal assistance and representation
i
children.text: by the Office of House Employment Counsel of the House of Representatives, in the case of assistance and representation in connection with a claim filed under subchapter IV (including all subsequent proceedings under such subchapter in connection with the claim) at a time when the chair of the Commission is a Member of the House, and in the case of assistance and representation in connection with any subsequent claim under subchapter IV related to the initial claim where the subsequent claim involves the same parties; or

children.label: ii
children.text: by the Office of Senate Chief Counsel for Employment of the Senate, in the case of assistance and representation in connection with a claim filed under subchapter IV (including all subsequent proceedings under such subchapter in connection with the claim) at a time when the chair of the Commission is a Senator, and in the case of assistance and representation in connection with any subsequent claim under subchapter IV related to the initial claim where the subsequent claim involves the same parties.

children.label: B
children.text: Legal assistance and representation under this chapter, including assistance and representation with respect to the proposal or acceptance of the disposition of a claim under this chapter, shall be provided to the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom by the Office of Senate Chief Counsel for Employment of the Senate, in the case of assistance and representation in connection with a claim filed under subchapter IV (including all subsequent proceedings under such subchapter in connection with such claim).

children.label: A
children.text: the term “China Review Commission” means the United States-China Economic and Security Review Commission established under , as enacted into law by ;section 7002 of title 22section 1 of Public Law 106–398

children.label: B
children.text: the term “Congressional-Executive China Commission” means the Congressional–Executive Commission on the People’s Republic of China established under title III of the U.S.–China Relations Act of 2000 (; et seq.);Public Law 106–28622 U.S.C. 6911

children.label: C
children.text: the term “Helsinki Commission” means the Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe established under the Act entitled “An Act to establish a Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe”, approved (; et seq.); andJune 3, 1976Public Law 94–30422 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: D
children.text: the term “United States Commission on International Religious Freedom” means the Commission established under .section 6431 of title 22
